{{Quote|I edit comic books. I drink beer. I have yet to drink beer while editing comics, but a man can always dream.|Dave Land|<ref name="MySpace">{{WebCite|url=http://www.myspace.com/dvlnd|text=Dave Land|nolive=1|archivedate=20090812200858}}</ref>}}
'''Dave Land''' was the lead editor of ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] for approximately five years. He wrote and starred in short introductory strips in most of his issues of ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'', as well as ''[[Boba Fett: Agent of Doom]]''. These were penciled mainly by [[Lucas Marangon]], who also penciled for several of the stories in ''Star Wars Tales''.

During the course of these ''Tales'', he has been frozen in [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]] twice by [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], once for attempting to remove Fett's helmet, and once as payback for the freezing in carbonite Fett himself suffered in the ''[[Star Wars Infinities: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' (which Land wrote), where his carbonite-frozen form is used as a desk by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]]. On the latter occasion, Land pleads with Fett to let him go as the "Infinities Series is non-canon, and therefore never happened."
